name: aligned_zero_size
reason: Size-zero alignment should not hold for nonzero addresses; indicates missing guard on size > 0

name: arch_inv_empty_layers
code:
proof fn phi_2_arch_inv_empty_layers(a: Arch)
    requires
        a.layers.len() == 0,
    ensures
        a.inv(),
{
}
reason: An architecture with zero layers maps nothing and should not satisfy the invariant

name: nonstandard_entry_size_last_layer
code:
proof fn phi_3_nonstandard_entry_size_last_layer(a: Arch)
    requires
        a.inv(),
        a.layers.len() == 1,
        a.entry_size(0) == 7,
    ensures
        a.entry_size_is_next_layer_size(0),
{
}
reason: A non-power-of-two entry size (7 bytes) should not pass the layer-size consistency check

name: wellformed_unaligned_base
code:
proof fn phi_4_wellformed_unaligned_base(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.arch.inv(),
        d.arch.layers.len() > 0,
        d.arch.entry_size(0) > 1,
        d.layer == 0,
        d.entries.len() == d.arch.num_entries(0),
        d.base_vaddr == 1,
    ensures
        d.well_formed(),
{
}
reason: A directory with base_vaddr=1 (unaligned) should not be well-formed; alignment check is commented out

name: entry_base_equals_next_entry_base
code:
proof fn phi_5_entry_base_equals_next_entry_base(a: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        a.inv(),
        layer < a.layers.len(),
    ensures
        a.entry_base(layer, base, idx) == a.next_entry_base(layer, base, idx),
{
}
reason: Consecutive entry bases being equal would collapse distinct entries into zero-width regions
